concepts.title: Idèles and class formations
concepts.blurb: Idèle class groups package global and local arithmetic data in one object.

concepts.title: Hilbert class field
concepts.blurb: The Hilbert class field $H/K$ is the maximal unramified abelian extension; the Artin map identifies $\operatorname{Gal}(H/K)$ with the ideal class group $\operatorname{Cl}(K)$, so $[H:K]=h_K$.

concepts.title: Artin reciprocity map
concepts.blurb: The Artin map identifies abelianized Galois groups with arithmetic quotient groups.

concepts.title: Local and global class field theory
concepts.blurb: Ray class fields and local reciprocity provide explicit control of abelian extensions.

concepts.title: Takagi existence theorem
concepts.blurb: Every finite-index open subgroup of an idèle class group (equivalently, every congruence subgroup of a ray class group) is the kernel of the Artin map for some finite abelian extension — the class-field correspondence is a bijection.

concepts.title: Conductor-discriminant formula
concepts.blurb: For a finite abelian $L/K$ the discriminant factors as the product of the conductors of its 1-dimensional characters, $\mathfrak{d}_{L/K}=\prod_\chi \mathfrak{f}(\chi)$, tying ramification to the Artin conductor.
